title: RFE: skopeo should check if the image already exists in the destination registry
body:
skopeo copy docker://$SRC_REG/$img:$tag docker://$DEST_REG/$img:$tag

Currently skopeo will copy the image again if the image already exists in the destination registry. It would be nice if a check was done with the copy command so it would not copy images that already exists.
If such a check is implemented it should probably be possible to override it however.

comments.body:
Thanks for the report.

This is non-trivial in the general case (see #281 for a more specific variant of this suggestion), but probably reasonably easily possible for the simple cases.

Hey,

 I have also noticed that all image layers are copied to the destination registry even if they already exist there. This is a big problem when copying images all over the world. Is there an estimate on when this will be implemented? Is this what this issue is about?

> I have also noticed that all image layers are copied to the destination registry even if they already exist there

Noticed how?  What are the exact steps of reproduce? Ordinarily pre-existing layers should not be redundantly copied, at least as long as the destination can tell the client that the layer is already present.

This issue seems more focused on the overhead of copying configs & manifests, and the overhead necessary to even avoid determining that the layers are already present.

```
[root@localhost ~]# skopeo --version
skopeo version 0.1.23 commit: 24510500d48f15b52ddfed6972a2a56452ef16b6
[root@localhost ~]# skopeo copy docker://registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/etcd:latest docker://registry.example.com:5000/rhel7/etcd:latest

Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
[root@localhost ~]# skopeo copy docker://registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/etcd:latest docker://registry.example.com:5000/rhel7/etcd:latest
Getting image source signatures

Copying blob sha256:d2365ea888622f84a07a68ccd26c9a81b32242fe624f7b979ce30c2399a750ab

Copying config sha256:797a818d8f9e3393b8c44cde94bc2108530afa4adb119d81166e7a69dbd4ad1e
 0 B / 7.67 KB [---------------------------------------------------------------]
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
[root@localhost ~]# 
```

It would certainly be desirable to have this working with this type destination. Thanks.
@myllynen That seems unexpected. Can you file a separate issue for this non-working deduplication, and in there, provide the output of the two commands with `skopeo --debug copy …`, please?

@mtrmac This issue was filed exactly about what was demonstrated above so I'm not sure do we need another issue?

In any case, here's the debug output, now from Fedora 27 Beta / skopeo-1.24:

Thanks.

> This issue was filed exactly about what was demonstrated above so I'm not sure do we need another issue?

Because
> Ordinarily pre-existing layers should not be redundantly copied, at least as long as the destination can tell the client that the layer is already present.
>
> This issue seems more focused on the overhead of copying configs & manifests, and the overhead necessary to even avoid determining that the layers are already present.

… and fixing the broken layer deduplication which is _already_ supposed to be working, and writing new code to avoid a copy of the entire image only by checking that the manifest is present, are quite different tasks.

Filed https://github.com/containers/image/issues/367 , please let’s discuss that there.
I believe this is fixed in the current skopeo.  Reopen if I am mistaken
Not _quite_, we still do the existence check layer by layer, but with the blob info cache it might be good enough.
